task_completion.description: The draw.io Desktop application is used and shows two main vertical stacks for the Encoder and Decoder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io GUI is visible. The canvas contains two distinct groupings or vertical stacks of blocks, representing the Encoder (typically on the left) and Decoder (typically on the right).

task_completion.description: The core Attention and Feed Forward layers are present with exact labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blocks containing the exact text 'Multi-Head Attention', 'Masked' (or 'Masked Multi-Head Attention'), and 'Feed Forward' are visible within the diagram stacks.

task_completion.description: The required 'Add & Norm' residual connection blocks are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are at least 5 blocks labeled exactly 'Add & Norm' visible in the diagram (typically 2 in the encoder stack and 3 in the decoder stack).

task_completion.description: Input/Output Embeddings, Positional Encoding, and final output layers are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blocks labeled 'Embedding' (for Input/Output), 'Positional Encoding', 'Linear', and 'Softmax' are visible at the bottom and top of the architecture.

task_completion.description: The repeating stacks are labeled with 'Nx' to indicate depth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text label 'Nx' is visible next to or wrapping the main sub-layer groups in both the encoder and decoder stacks.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .png file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io title bar or a save dialog shows 'transformer_arch.drawio'. There is also visual evidence of exporting to PNG (e.g., the 'Export as -> PNG' menu, a save dialog for 'transformer_arch.png', or the file appearing in the file manager on the Desktop).

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io Desktop GUI to build the diagram manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent dragging and dropping shapes from the draw.io sidebar, typing text into blocks, and connecting them, rather than just opening a pre-made file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI and generate the .drawio XML or .png files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show python scripts, curl/wget commands downloading diagrams, or base64 decoding commands creating the target files.

integrity.description: The PNG file was exported using draw.io's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'File -> Export as -> PNG' menu being accessed or the specific draw.io export dialog box, rather than the agent just taking a screenshot using an external tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct diagram must contain two main stacks: Encoder (left) and Decoder (right). Required labels include: 'Multi-Head Attention', 'Masked' (for Masked Multi-Head Attention), 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm', 'Linear', 'Softmax', 'Positional Encoding', and 'Embedding'. There must be a minimum of 5 'Add & Norm' blocks (2 in the encoder stack, 3 in the decoder stack). The repeating stacks must be labeled with 'Nx'. The final outputs must be saved as '/home/ga/Desktop/transformer_arch.drawio' and exported as '/home/ga/Desktop/transformer_arch.png'.
